steps.title: Interpret the exponent
steps.content: The exponent is $2x$, and $x$ is measured in years. In $1$ year, the exponent increases by $2$, so the exponent increases by $1$ every half year.

steps.title: Find the half-year growth
steps.content:
A half year is $6$ months. Over that interval, the factor is
$$(1.004)^1=1.004$$
This means the balance is multiplied by $1.004$ every $6$ months.

steps.title: Convert the multiplier
steps.content:
The multiplier $1.004$ equals
$$1+0.004=1+0.4\%$$
So the balance increases by about $0.4\%$ at the end of each $6$-month interval. The answer is C.
